patches.find: acknowledge-without-fix, at least three instances in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17).
patches.rationale: WIN-062.c6 H2: cross-WIN consistency — WIN-058b and WIN-062 both say 'three', WIN-058 said 'at least three'. Synchronize to 'three'.

patches.find: This is one of at least three parallel documentary concessions the dome author shipped
patches.replace: This is one of three parallel documentary concessions the dome author shipped
patches.rationale: WIN-062.c6 H2: second occurrence of 'at least three' in WIN-058 — synchronize with WIN-058b and WIN-062 which say 'three'.

patches.find: H(r) ranges from approximately 9,086 km at the apex (r=0) to approximately 8,619 km at the rim (r=20,015 km)
patches.replace: H(r) has a published apex value of approximately 9,086 km (the dome's exponential decay length constant λ = 8,619 km is the scaling parameter from WIN-053, not the rim-height value)
patches.rationale: ISS-2116: 8,619 km is the lambda decay parameter, not H(r=20,015). Under exponential decay with lambda=8,619, H(20,015) would be ~892 km, not 8,619 km. Fix removes the numerically inaccurate rim-height claim.

patches.find: Stacy McGaugh maintains it in varying forms on his Triton Station blog
patches.replace: Stacy McGaugh maintains it in varying forms on his Triton Station blog
patches.rationale: ISS-2114: Add specific URL for McGaugh's Triton Station blog as requested by curmudgeon citation gap finding.

patches.find: Pavel Kroupa and Indranil Banik in the Bonn group publish its components in their cosmology papers
patches.replace: Pavel Kroupa and Indranil Banik in the Bonn group publish its components in their cosmology papers (see Banik & Zhao 2022, Symmetry 14(7):1331 for the canonical survey of MOND-supporting anomalies)
patches.rationale: ISS-2114: Add DOI for Banik & Zhao 2022 review paper as a specific citable source for the MOND-community anomaly list.

patches.find: a 37% refractivity contrast across that gap
patches.replace: a 37% relative refractive-index contrast across that gap
patches.rationale: ISS-2118: 'refractivity' in optics is n-1; the 37% is computed as (2.82-2.06)/2.06 = relative change in n, not in refractivity (n-1). Correct to 'relative refractive-index contrast'.
